facts.text: Levittown officially named on Jan 1, 1948, marking the start of America’s first mass‑produced suburban community.
Homer Levitt’s company built thousands of ranch‑style homes in the 1950s, selling each for about $8,000—roughly $115,000 today.
The Long Island Rail Road serves Levittown via Hicksville and Bethpage stations on the Main Line and Wantagh and Bellmore on the Babylon Branch.
Billy Joel, the famed pianist, grew up in a Levitt‑built house in nearby Hicksville, a testament to the area’s influence on music.
Levitt & Sons faced picket lines when they used non‑union contractors, sparking early labor disputes in suburban construction.
The 1994 documentary “Building The American Dream: Levittown, NY” captures the community’s rapid growth and includes interviews with residents and the founder.
